title: 第 62 節
en: 53 (171). O Maker of the material world, thou Holy One ! When are sweetness and fatness to come back again to that land and to those fields, with health and healing, with fulness and increase and growth, and a growing of corn and grass ?

54j 55 (172)- Ahura Mazda answered : * Sweetness and fatness will never come back again to that land and to those fields, with health and healing, with fulness and increase and growth, and a growing of corn and grass, until the murderer of the water-dog has been smitten to death on the spot, and the holy soul of the dog has been offered up a sacrifice, for three days and three nights, with fire blazing, with Baresma tied up, and with Haoma prepared ^.

56 (174). ['Then sweetness and fatness will come back again to that land and to those fields, with health and healing, with fulness and increase and growth, and a growing of corn and grass *.'] Fargard XIV.

This Fargard is nothing more than an appendix to the last clauses in the preceding Fargard (§ 50 seq.) * How the murder of a water-dog (an otter) may be atoned for is described in it at full length. The extravagance of the penalties prescribed may well make it doubtful whether the legislation of the Vendidad had ever any substantial existence in practice. These exorbitant prescriptions seem to be intended only to impress on the mind of the faithful the heinousness of the offence to be avoided.
' See p. 136, n. i. * Cf. Farg. IX, 53-57.
1 70 vendIdAd.

I. Zarathurtra asked Ahura Mazda : * O Ahura Mazda, most beneficent Spirit, Maker of the material world, thou Holy One ! He who smites one of those water-dogs that are bom one from a thousand dogs and a thousand she-dogs ^ so that he gives up the ghost and the soul parts from the body, what is the penalty that he shall pay ? '
2 (4). Ahura Mazda answered : * He shall pay ten thousand stripes with the Aspah^-artra, ten thousand stripes with the Sraosh6-^rana *.
' He shall godly and piously bring unto the fire of Ahura Mazda ' ten thousand loads of hard, well dried, well examined^ wood, to redeem his own soul.

3 (6). * He shall godly and piously bring unto the fire of Ahura Mazda ten thousand loads of soft wood, of Urvdsna, Vohfl-gaona, VohA-kereti, Hadhftna^pata •, or any sweet-scented plant, to redeem his own .souL 4 {7). * He shall godly and piously tie ten thousand bundles of Baresma, to redeem his own soul.
Avesta; if he cannot afford it, it will be suflScient to perform a complete Izanifi (sacrifice),' (Comm.)
' To the altar of the Bahr&m fire.

kindle it thereon ; it is forbidden to kindle green wood, and even though the wood were hard and dry, one must examine it three times, lest there may be any hair or any unclean matter upon it ' (Gr. Rav.) Although the pious Ar^ Vfdlf had always taken the utmost care never to put on the fire any wood but such as was seven years old, yet, when he entered Paradise, Atar, the genius of fire, showed him reproachfuDy a large tank full of the water which that wood had exuded (see AtA Vir&f X).

FAKGARD XIV. I7I sand Zaothra libations with the Haoma and the milk, cleanly prepared and well strained, cleanly prepared and well strained by a pious man, and mixed with the roots of the tree known as HadhA-nafipata, to redeem his own soul.

5 (9). * He shall kill ten thousand snakes of those that go upon the belly. He shall kill ten thousand Kahrpus, who are snakes with the shape of a dog ^ He shall kill ten thousand tortoises ^ He shall kill ten thousand land-frogs*; he shall kill ten thousand waterfrogs. H e shall kill ten thousand corn-carrying ants * ;

^ 'Mir binak snakes: they are dog-like, because they sit on their hindparts ' (Comm.) The cat (gurba=Kahrpu) seems to be the animal intended. In a paraphrase of this passage in a Parsi Ravdet, the cat is nmnbered amongst the Khrafstras which it is enjoined to kill to redeem a sin (India Office Library, VllI, 13); cf. G. du Chinon, p. 46a : 'Les animaux que les Gaures ont en horreur sont les serpents, les couleuvrcs, les lezars, et autres de cette espece, les crapaux, ks grenouVlles, les ^crevisses, les rats et souris, et sur tout le chat.'
» Cf. Farg. XIII, 6-j.

• * Those that can go out of water and live on the dry ground ' (Comm.) ' Pour les grenoulflles et crapaux, ils disent que ce sont ceux (eux ?) qui sont cause de ce que les hommes meurent, gdtans les eaus oh ils habitent continuellement, et que d'autant plus qu'il y en a dans le pafs, d'autant plus les eaus causent-elles des maladies et enlin la mort,' G. du Chinon, p. 465.

^ Herodotus already mentions the war waged by the Magi against snakes and ants (I, 140). — 'Un jour que j'^tois surpris de la guerre qu'ils font aux fourmis, ils me dirent que ces animaux ne faisaient que voler par des amas des grains plus qu'il n'^toit n^cessaire pour leur nourriture,' G« du Chinon, p. 464. Firdausi protested against the proscription: 'Do no harm to the corncarrying ant ; a living thing it is, and its life is dear to it/ The celebrated high-priest of the Parsis, the late Mooki Firooz, entered those lines into his Pand N&mah, which may betoken better days for the wise little creature.

he shall kill ten thousand ants of the small, venomous mischievous kind ^ 6 (i6). * He shall kill ten thousand worms of those that live on dirt; he shall kill ten thousand raging flies \ clean \ twice the set of seven implements for the fire*, to redeem his own soul, namely : —

7 (20). * The two answering implements for fire" ; a broom ' ; a pair of tongs ; a pair of round bellows extended at the bottom, contracted at the top; a sharp-edged sharp-pointed* adze; a sharp-toothed sharp-pointed saw ; by means of which the worshippers of Mazda procure wood for the fire of Ahura Mazda.

8 (26). * He shall godly and piously give to godly men a set of the priestly instruments of which the priests make use, to redeem his own soul, namely : The A^tra • ; the meat-vessel ; the Paitidina ^® ; the ^ Perhaps: 'of the small, venomous kind, with a mischievous track ' (Bund. XIX, a8 : * when the grain-carrier travels over the earth, ii produces a hollow track : when the hedgehog travels over it, the track goes away from it and it becomes level : ' cf. Farg. XIII, 2, note).

^ To cleanse the Atash-din or flre-vessel (Yasna IX, 1).

'^ As everything that goes out of man is unclean, his breath defiles all that it touches ; priests, therefore, while on duty, and even laymen, while praying or eating, must wear a mouth-veil, the FARGARD XIV. I 73 Khrafstraghna ^ ; the Sraosh6->farana ^ ; the cup for the Myazda'; the cups for mixing and dividing*; the regular mortar*; the Haoma cups®; and the Baresma.
9 (32). 'He shall godly and piously give to godly men a set of all the war implements of which the warriors make use, to redeem his own soul ;
third a club, the fourth a bow, the fifth a saddle with a quiver and thirty brass-headed arrows, the sixth a sling with arm-string and with thirty sling stones '' ;
ninth a tunic*, the tenth a helmet, the eleventh a girdle, the twelfth a pair of greaves.

ID (41). * He shall godly and piously give to godly men a set of all the implements of which the Paitiddna (Parsi Pcndm), consisting 'of two pieces of white cotton cloth, hanging loosely from the bridge of the nose to, at least, two inches below the mouth, and tied with two strings at the back of the head ' (Haug, Essays, and ed. p. 243, n. i ; cf. Comm. ad Farg. XVIII, i, and Anquetil II, 530), It is a stick with a leather thong at its end, something like the Indian fly- flap.
zh: 物質世界的造物者啊，聖潔者！何時甘美與肥沃將重新降臨於彼地彼田，伴以健康與療癒、豐饒與增長、以及五穀與草木的生長？
阿胡拉‧瑪茲達回答說：「甜蜜與肥沃將永遠不會回到那片土地與那些田野，伴隨著健康與治癒、充裕與增長及生長，以及穀物與草的生長，直到殺害水犬者在原地被擊殺至死，且該犬的聖魂已獻祭於三日三夜之中，有熊熊烈火焚燒，有婆羅斯瑪（Baresma）綑綁，有豪瑪（Haoma）備妥。」
56 (174). 【「那時，甜蜜與肥沃將重臨彼土與彼田地，伴隨健康與療癒、豐盛與增長及生成，以及穀物與青草之增長*。」】《法爾加德第十四篇》(Fargard XIV)。
本法捲（Fargard）不過是前一法捲末尾條款（§ 50 及以下）* 的附錄。其中詳細描述了如何為殺害水犬（水獺）的罪行進行贖罪。所規定的處罰之過度性質，實令人懷疑《萬德達》的立法是否曾在實踐中具有實質的存在。這些過度的規定似乎只是旨在向信徒心中印刻該應迴避之罪行的嚴重性。
見第一百三十六頁，註一。* 參見《法爾加德經》第九卷，第五十三至五十七節。
梨俱吠陀第一卷第七十讚。
一、瑣羅亞斯德問阿胡拉‧馬茲達：「啊，阿胡拉‧馬茲達，至仁至善的精靈，物質世界的造物者，聖哉者也！凡擊殺彼等水犬者——乃由千隻公犬與千隻母犬中生出之犬——使其氣絕身亡，靈魂離於肉體，此人應當付出何等之罪罰？」
2（4）。阿胡拉‧瑪茲達回答：「他應當以阿斯帕赫-阿爾特拉（Aspah-artra）鞭打一萬次，以斯拉歐什塔-卡拉納（Sraosh6-^rana）鞭打一萬次。」
「他應當虔敬、恭謹地向阿胡拉‧馬茲達（Ahura Mazda）的聖火獻上一萬擔堅硬、妥善風乾、仔細審查的木材，以救贖他自己的靈魂。」
3 (6). * 他應當虔誠且敬畏地向阿胡拉‧馬茲達（Ahura Mazda）的火堆獻上一萬捆柔軟的木材，取自烏爾瓦茲納（Urvdsna）、沃赫爾‧蓋奧納（Vohfl-gaona）、沃赫爾‧克雷蒂（VohA-kereti）、哈德赫蒂納帕塔（Hadhftna^pata）或任何芬芳的植物，以救贖自己的靈魂。4 (7). * 他應當虔誠且敬畏地綁紮一萬束巴雷斯瑪（Baresma），以救贖自己的靈魂。
《阿維斯陀》；如果他無法負擔，完成一場完整的伊札尼菲（Izanifi，祭祀）就足夠了。（註疏）
轉向巴赫拉姆火焰的祭壇。
在其上點燃它；禁止點燃溼木，即便木材乾硬，亦須檢查三次，恐怕其上有毛髮或任何不潔物質（格拉茲‧拉比）。儘管虔誠的阿里‧瓦哈夫一向小心謹慎，只在火上放置七年以上的木材，然而當他進入天堂時，火的精靈阿塔爾卻譴責地向他展示了一個裝滿該木材滲出之水的大水槽（見《阿塔‧維拉夫》第十章）。
法克加德第十四章。一百七十一則以郝馬及牛乳進行薩奧特拉灑奠，潔淨備製且妥善過濾，由虔誠之人潔淨備製且妥善過濾，並與名為哈德哈─那匹帕塔（HadhA-nafipata）的樹根混合，以救贖其靈魂。
（9）。*他將殺死一萬條腹行之蛇。他將殺死一萬個卡赫普斯（Kahrpus），即具有狗形的蛇。^他將殺死一萬隻烏龜。^他將殺死一萬隻陸地青蛙；他將殺死一萬隻水青蛙。他將殺死一萬隻負穀之蟻。*

^ 「米爾比納克蛇：牠們是狗一樣的，因為牠們坐在後腿上」(評註)。貓(gurba=Kahrpu)似乎是指的動物。在一部拜火教羅達葉的此段落的釋文中，貓被列在克拉夫斯特拉斯(Khrafstras)之中，據規定應當殺死牠們以贖罪(印度辦公室圖書館，第八冊，第13頁)；參見G. du Chinon, p. 46a：「高爾人(Gaures)所厭惡的動物乃是蛇、蛇類、蜥蜴及同類生物、蟾蜍、青蛙、螯蝦、鼠類，尤其是貓。」
» 參見《法爾迦德》第十三章第6節。
「那些能夠離開水域並在乾地上生活的東西」（註解）「關於青蛙和蟾蜍，他們說這些是導致人類死亡的原因，因為它們不斷棲居在水中，而且一個地區裡數量越多，水就越容易引發疾病，最終導致死亡」，杜‧希農著，第465頁。

^ 赫羅多德已在其著作中提及祆教祭司（Magi）對蛇和螞蟻所發動的戰爭（第一卷，第140章）。——「有一天我對他們對螞蟻所進行的戰爭感到驚訝，他們告訴我，這些動物只是盜取了超過牠們所需食物以上的穀物堆積」，G《Chinon論》，第464頁。菲爾道西曾對此禁令提出異議：「勿傷運穀的螞蟻；牠是一個生靈，生命對牠而言珍貴無比。」祆教著名的大祭司、已故的穆基‧菲羅茲將這些詩句收錄於其《Pand Nāmah》中，這可能預示著這位聰慧的小生靈將迎來更好的時代。
我注意到您提供的「英文原文」只有「172 vendidAd.」這一行文字。 這似乎不是完整的英文段落，而是一個頁碼標記和可能的阿維斯陀文獻（Vendidad）的參考。這不足以進行學術翻譯。 請提供完整的英文原文段落，我才能按照您的規則進行準確的繁體中文翻譯。
他應殺死一萬隻小型、有毒、作亂的螞蟻^6（i6）。*他應殺死一萬條居住在污垢中的蟲；他應殺死一萬隻凶惡的蒼蠅\潔淨\火的七件工具的雙倍集合*，以救贖他自己的靈魂，即：——
7 (20). *兩件相應的工具用於生火*；一把掃帚；一對火鉗；一對下端寬闊、上端收縮的圓形風箱；一把邊利尖銳的斧頭；一把齒利尖銳的鋸子；藉由這些工具，瑪茲達的信徒們為阿胡拉‧瑪茲達的聖火備好木柴。
8 (26). * 他應當虔誠而恭敬地將一套祭司所使用的聖祭儀器贈予虔誠的人，以救贖其自身之靈魂，即：Atra；肉器；Paitidina；^ 或許：「小的、有毒的種類，具有惡意的軌跡」(Bund. XIX, a8：「當穀物運輸者在大地上行走時，它產生一道凹陷的軌跡；當刺蝟在其上行走時，軌跡遠離它而大地變得平坦：」參閱 Farg. XIII, 2, note)。
法格特經（Farg. IX, 6 seq.）
^ 淨化阿塔什-丁（Atash-din）或火器皿（Yasna IX, 1）。
由於凡是從人身上發出的東西都是不潔的，他的呼吸玷污了它所接觸的一切；因此，執行職務的祭司，甚至祈禱或進食中的俗人，都必須戴上口罩，即 Khrafstraghna；Sraosh6->farana；祭獻用的杯；混合和分配用的杯；常規的研缽；Haoma 杯；及 Baresma。
9 (32). 他應當虔誠地、虔敬地將戰士所使用的各式戰爭器械悉數贈予虔誠的人，以救贖自己的靈魂；
第三件是木棍，第四件是弓，第五件是鞍和箭筒及三十支銅頭箭，第六件是投石器及臂帶和三十顆投石用的石子''；
第九件是長衣（tunic），第十件是頭盔，第十一件是腰帶，第十二件是脛甲。

ID (41). * 他應當虔誠而恭敬地賜予虔誠的人一整套所有的儀式用具，其中包括帕伊蒂達那（Paitiddna，祆教聖巾），由「兩片白棉布組成，從鼻樑處鬆散地垂掛下來，至少延伸到嘴下兩英寸處，並在頭後用兩條繩子繫牢」（Haug，Essays，第二版，第243頁，注1；參閱 Comm. ad Farg. XVIII, i 及 Anquetil II, 530）。這是一根木棍，末端附有皮繩，有點像印度的驅蠅拂。
